{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# The Attention Mechanism {#sec-attention}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- From the graph intuition to the concrete operation: queries, keys,
     values, scaled dot products, multiple heads, and the causal mask. -->
:::

**Learning objectives.**

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]
- [ ]

## Queries, keys, and values

<!-- Three learned projections of the residual stream. What each one is for. -->

## Scaled dot-product attention

<!-- The softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k)) V formula; derive the 1/sqrt(d_k) scaling.
     Reference: `src/thebook/attention.py`. -->

## Multi-head attention

<!-- Splitting into subspaces; heads as parallel relation types; concat and
     project. Parameter and FLOP accounting. -->

## Causal masking

<!-- Autoregression as a directed graph; the lower-triangular mask; why it
     preserves the training-parallel / inference-sequential asymmetry. -->

## Complexity

<!-- The O(n^2) cost in sequence length — the seed for @sec-hardware-lottery
     and Part V. -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO: implement scaled_dot_product_attention and MHA from the stubs. -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}

<!-- [@vaswani2017attention] -->
